title: Dead Lettering, Poison Messages, and Parking · Runtime Contract: Dead Letter Cycle
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must interpret x-death evidence defensively, prevent cycles, retain bounded context, and authorize replay through a separate path; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dead letter cycle increment in a Go poison-message remediation workflow. Learners progress t…
objectives: Detect and bound dead-letter cycles using history, stable identity, attempt policy, route analysis, and a terminal parking destination; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Configure dead lettering through mutable policy where possible and verify source read, target write, exchange existence, binding, and route permissions; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Dead Letter Cycle · Theory Dead Letter Cycle
steps.instruction:
